rows.acceptance: Add integration harness + docs (`docs/ECONOMY.md`, `docs/IDENTITY.md`, `docs/DATA_SCOPE_BOUNDARIES.md`) plus contract check `systems/ops/data_scope_boundary_check.{ts,js}` to enforce: user-specific contracts/preferences/signature state only in `memory/` + `adaptive/`, permanent policy/runtime code only in `systems/` + `config/`; integrate lanes with soul vector, tithe engine, spawn broker, guard, fractal, complexity warden, and jigsaw receipts without boundary violations.

rows.title: CRDT Local-First State Plane (Soul/Memory/Contract Domains)
rows.problem: Current state lanes are resilient but not yet modeled as canonical CRDT local-first domains with deterministic conflict semantics across offline windows.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/pinnacle/crdt_state_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/crdt_state_plane_policy.json` with per-domain CRDT schemas and reconciliation rules; user-specific CRDT documents/anchors persist under `memory/crdt/` and adaptive merge heuristics under `adaptive/crdt/`; permanent merge logic/schemas stay in `systems/pinnacle/` + `config/`; sync emits JetStream reconciliation receipts with replay parity checks.

rows.title: WASM Component Runtime for Skills/Habits
rows.problem: Language-agnostic secure module execution requires capability-scoped sandboxing and signed components, not only script-level contracts.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/wasm/component_runtime.{ts,js}` + policy `config/wasm_component_runtime_policy.json` with signature verification, capability manifests, and deny-by-default host bindings; user-specific module preferences/overrides live in `memory/wasm/` and adaptive module ranking/cooldowns in `adaptive/wasm/`; permanent runtime/verification logic remains in `systems/wasm/` + `config/`.

rows.title: Intent Declaration + Translation Plane
rows.problem: Intent exists implicitly in objectives/contracts but lacks a first-class machine-readable declaration-to-execution contract with auditable translation evidence.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/intent/intent_translation_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/intent_translation_policy.json`; user intents/preferences persist under `memory/intent/` and adaptive ranking/disambiguation state under `adaptive/intent/`; permanent parsers, planning rules, and safety translators remain in `systems/intent/` + `config/`; every translation emits explainable intent->plan receipts and risk-tier gates.

rows.title: DID + Verifiable Credential Soul Binding Layer
rows.problem: Soul authority exists, but standards-aligned DID/VC issuance and verification are needed for interoperable sovereign identity proofs.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/identity/did_vc_binding.{ts,js}` + policy `config/did_vc_binding_policy.json`; user DID docs/credentials are stored in `memory/identity/did/` and adaptive trust/routing preferences in `adaptive/identity/did/`; permanent issuance/verification schemas and revocation logic stay in `systems/identity/` + `config/`; credentials for badges/contracts/lineage are verifiable and auditable without exposing non-required private fields.

rows.title: Content-Addressed Archival Plane (IPFS-Compatible)
rows.problem: Long-term continuity exists, but content-addressed retrieval proofs and pinning contracts are needed for immutable, portable archival semantics.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/archival/content_addressed_archive.{ts,js}` + policy `config/content_addressed_archive_policy.json` with CID mapping, pin metadata, retrieval verification, and encrypted payload envelopes; user-specific archival payload/index references live under `memory/archival/content_addressed/` with adaptive pin/cache heuristics under `adaptive/archival/`; permanent addressing/protocol logic remains in `systems/archival/` + `config/`.

rows.title: Zero-Knowledge Compliance Proof Layer
rows.problem: Compliance proofs currently rely on direct evidence disclosure, which increases privacy exposure for high-sensitivity economic/contract lanes.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/crypto/zk_compliance_proofs.{ts,js}` (Rust-backed where practical) + policy `config/zk_compliance_policy.json`; user-specific witness material and proof requests persist under `memory/crypto/zk/` and adaptive proof-selection heuristics under `adaptive/crypto/zk/`; permanent circuits, verifiers, and policy checks stay in `systems/crypto/` + `config/`; proofs verify contract-compliance claims with bounded disclosure and deterministic verification receipts.

rows.title: FHE Encrypted Compute Pilot Lane
rows.problem: Encrypted-state compute is a strategic sovereignty advantage but requires bounded rollout due to performance and operational complexity constraints.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/crypto/fhe_encrypted_compute_pilot.{ts,js}` + policy `config/fhe_encrypted_compute_policy.json` with allowlisted operations, latency/cost budget gates, fallback contracts, and deterministic failure handling; user-specific encrypted operation payloads remain under `memory/crypto/fhe/` and adaptive operator selection under `adaptive/crypto/fhe/`; permanent evaluators/keys-policy/runtime code remain in `systems/crypto/` + `config/`.

rows.title: Pinnacle Integration Contract + Data-Scope Guard
rows.problem: Independent pinnacle lanes can create boundary drift unless cross-organ contracts and storage-scope invariants are continuously enforced.
rows.acceptance: Implement integrated harness/docs (`docs/PINNACLE_TECH.md`, updates to `docs/SECURITY.md` and `docs/MIND_SOVEREIGNTY.md`) and `systems/ops/pinnacle_integration_contract_check.{ts,js}` to enforce cross-lane compatibility, risk-tier gating, JetStream publication, and data-scope boundaries (`memory/adaptive` user-only, `systems/config/docs` permanent-only) across CRDT/WASM/Intent/DID/archival/ZK/FHE lanes.

rows.title: Actor Runtime Substrate (Isolated Swarm Message-Passing)
rows.problem: Existing orchestration is resilient but not yet unified under one actor-model contract for isolated state and deterministic mailbox semantics.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/runtime/actor_runtime_substrate.{ts,js}` (Rust-backed where appropriate) + policy `config/actor_runtime_policy.json`; user-specific actor preferences/limits persist under `memory/runtime/actors/` with adaptive scheduling heuristics under `adaptive/runtime/actors/`; permanent actor runtime/mailbox/supervisor logic remains in `systems/runtime/` + `config/` with JetStream message-flow receipts.

rows.title: Solid Pod Interop Bridge (User-Owned Data Pods)
rows.problem: Sovereign local storage exists, but standards-based pod interoperability is needed for portable user-controlled data exchange across ecosystems.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/federation/solid_pod_bridge.{ts,js}` + policy `config/solid_pod_bridge_policy.json`; user-specific pod endpoints/tokens/mappings stay under `memory/federation/solid/` and adaptive sync-health heuristics under `adaptive/federation/solid/`; permanent protocol adapters, schema mappers, and trust policy remain in `systems/federation/` + `config/`.

rows.title: ActivityPub + Matrix Federation Gateway
rows.problem: Decentralized inter-instance communication requires canonical protocol gateways with bounded trust and policy-aware routing.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/federation/activitypub_matrix_gateway.{ts,js}` + policy `config/activitypub_matrix_gateway_policy.json`; user-specific federation peers/allowlists/preferences remain under `memory/federation/channels/` and adaptive reliability/rate heuristics under `adaptive/federation/channels/`; permanent federation transport/bridge logic remains in `systems/federation/` + `config/` with egress/audit receipts.

rows.title: Edge/Fog Offload Orchestrator (Edge-First Compute Placement)
rows.problem: Heavy workloads need explicit placement contracts (local/edge/borrowed) to reduce latency and increase resilience under variable capacity.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/hardware/edge_fog_offload_orchestrator.{ts,js}` + policy `config/edge_fog_offload_policy.json`; user-specific offload preferences/constraints persist under `memory/compute/offload/` and adaptive placement telemetry under `adaptive/compute/offload/`; permanent placement schedulers and admission policy stay in `systems/hardware/` + `config/` with deterministic fallback receipts.

rows.title: Real-Time Collaboration Presence Engine
rows.problem: Base CRDT replication does not fully cover multi-user presence/session semantics and high-frequency collaborative editing ergonomics.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/collaboration/realtime_presence_engine.{ts,js}` + policy `config/realtime_presence_policy.json`; user-specific room/session membership and collaboration preferences remain in `memory/collaboration/` with adaptive collaboration QoS tuning under `adaptive/collaboration/`; permanent session orchestration, conflict semantics, and transport contracts stay in `systems/collaboration/` + `config/`.

rows.title: Spatial Computing Abstraction Layer
rows.problem: Future-facing interfaces need a standard spatial primitive layer before concrete AR/VR client implementations can be safely composed.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/spatial/spatial_abstraction_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `config/spatial_abstraction_policy.json`; user-specific spatial preferences/profiles stay in `memory/spatial/` with adaptive layout/interaction tuning under `adaptive/spatial/`; permanent spatial scene/action contracts remain in `systems/spatial/` + `config/`, integrated with visual signature lanes.

rows.title: AI-Native Multimodel Data Plane
rows.problem: Current storage is effective but fragmented; a unified multimodel plane is needed for consistent vector/graph/document/relational semantics under one governed contract.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/data/ai_multimodel_data_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/ai_multimodel_data_plane_policy.json`; user-specific query/view preferences persist under `memory/data/preferences/` with adaptive indexing/routing heuristics under `adaptive/data/`; permanent schema/runtime/migration contracts remain in `systems/data/` + `config/`; rollout includes reversible migration + parity receipts against existing memory/runtime lanes.

rows.title: WebGPU + Rust Client Compute Plane
rows.problem: High-cost local visual/fractal workloads benefit from GPU acceleration and client-side Rust compute while preserving sovereign local execution guarantees.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/client/webgpu_rust_compute_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/webgpu_rust_compute_policy.json`; user-specific client compute profiles/preferences persist under `memory/client_compute/` with adaptive performance heuristics under `adaptive/client_compute/`; permanent compute kernels/runtime bindings remain in `systems/client/` + `config/`, with deterministic fallback to CPU paths and receipted performance deltas.

rows.title: Collective Training Orchestrator (Crowd GPU Distributed Training Plane)
rows.problem: Existing specialist training lanes are strong but not yet unified into a crowd-GPU orchestrator spanning tithe and peer-lending compute pools.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/training/collective_training_orchestrator.{ts,js}` (Rust worker optional) + policy `config/collective_training_orchestrator_policy.json`; user-specific contribution preferences and participation caps persist under `memory/training/contributions/` with adaptive scheduling/allocator heuristics under `adaptive/training/contributions/`; permanent orchestration, cluster scheduling, and job governance remain in `systems/training/` + `config/` with deterministic run receipts.

rows.title: Soul-Contract Training Contribution & Settlement Layer
rows.problem: Compute contributors need explicit governed agreements/reward settlement to keep incentives aligned and auditable at scale.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/training/training_contribution_contracts.{ts,js}` + policy `config/training_contribution_contract_policy.json`; user agreements/signatures/terms remain in `memory/training/contracts/` with adaptive contributor-scoring under `adaptive/training/contracts/`; permanent settlement/reward logic remains in `systems/training/` + `systems/economy/` + `config/`; all settlements publish event-stream receipts and are verifiable against contribution telemetry.

rows.title: Fractal Curriculum + Evaluation Oversight for Collective Training
rows.problem: Distributed training quality degrades without a governed loop for data curation, curriculum mutation, and checkpoint evidence gates.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/training/fractal_training_oversight.{ts,js}` + policy `config/fractal_training_oversight_policy.json`; user-specific model preference signals remain in `memory/training/preferences/` with adaptive curriculum weighting in `adaptive/training/curriculum/`; permanent curation/eval/gating logic remains in `systems/training/` + `systems/fractal/` + `config/`; checkpoint promotions require bounded evidence packs and non-regression receipts.

rows.title: Sovereign Model Rollout Ladder (Tier-3 Governed + Fallback/Rollback)
rows.problem: Community-trained model updates can introduce regressions unless rollout includes strict governance, canary proof, and guaranteed fallback availability.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/training/sovereign_model_rollout_ladder.{ts,js}` + policy `config/sovereign_model_rollout_policy.json`; user-specific rollout pinning/opt-in preferences remain under `memory/training/rollout_preferences/` with adaptive risk scoring in `adaptive/training/rollout/`; permanent release/canary/rollback logic remains in `systems/training/` + `config/`; tier-3+ promotions require explicit approval receipts and automatic fallback contracts.

rows.title: Encrypted Model Artifact Archive (Content-Addressed Weights)
rows.problem: Model lineage and recovery need encrypted content-addressed artifact contracts beyond generic archival lanes.
rows.acceptance: Extend archival contracts via `systems/training/model_artifact_archive.{ts,js}` + policy `config/model_artifact_archive_policy.json`; user-specific access permissions/entitlements remain under `memory/training/artifact_access/` with adaptive retrieval/caching hints in `adaptive/training/artifacts/`; permanent artifact packaging/encryption/addressing logic stays in `systems/training/` + `systems/archival/` + `config/`; every promoted model has verifiable encrypted archival receipts.

rows.title: Contributor Incentive Engine (Badges, Rewards, Recognition)
rows.problem: Sustainable collective training requires deterministic contributor recognition and reward contracts linked to real compute impact.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/economy/training_contributor_incentive_engine.{ts,js}` + policy `config/training_contributor_incentive_policy.json`; user-specific badge history and reward preferences remain in `memory/economy/training_rewards/` with adaptive contribution quality scoring in `adaptive/economy/training_rewards/`; permanent reward formulas/issuance logic remain in `systems/economy/` + `config/`; awards integrate with honor codex and emit auditable receipts.

rows.title: Access-Tier Governance for Sovereign Model (Free Base + Priority Tiers)
rows.problem: Universal base access improves adoption, but governance needs an explicit tiering contract to preserve contributor incentives and operational sustainability.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/economy/model_access_tier_governance.{ts,js}` + policy `config/model_access_tier_policy.json`; user-specific tier preferences/eligibility state remain under `memory/economy/model_access/` with adaptive queue/QoS tuning in `adaptive/economy/model_access/`; permanent access policy, fairness rules, and budget controls remain in `systems/economy/` + `config/`; base-free and priority-tier semantics are enforced with receipts and fail-safe fallback to base access.

rows.title: Collective Intelligence Integration Contract + Data-Scope Guard
rows.problem: Training, economy, identity, and archival lanes can drift without integrated contract checks and explicit storage-boundary enforcement.
rows.acceptance: Implement integrated checks/docs via `systems/ops/collective_intelligence_contract_check.{ts,js}`, update `docs/ECONOMY.md` and new `docs/INTELLIGENCE.md`; enforce data boundaries (`memory/adaptive` user-only, `systems/config/docs` permanent-only) and cross-lane invariants across orchestrator, contracts, fractal oversight, rollout, archival, incentives, and access tiers with deterministic regression receipts.

rows.title: Git-Backed Memory Filesystem (MemFS Contract)
rows.problem: Memory provenance and branchable replay need first-class versioned filesystem semantics beyond current artifact snapshots.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/memfs_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `config/memfs_layer_policy.json`; user-specific memory documents/history remain under `memory/fs/` and adaptive retrieval ranking/merge hints under `adaptive/memory/fs/`; permanent memfs orchestration/git-worktree contracts remain in `systems/memory/` + `config/`; commits are policy-annotated and replay-verifiable.

rows.title: Sleep Reflection Compute Scheduler (Idle Consolidation Loop)
rows.problem: Memory and strategy quality compounds faster with bounded asynchronous reflection during idle windows.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/sleep_reflection_scheduler.{ts,js}` + policy `config/sleep_reflection_scheduler_policy.json`; user-specific reflection outputs stay in `memory/reflections/` and adaptive scheduling heuristics in `adaptive/reflections/`; permanent scheduler/executor logic stays in `systems/memory/` + `systems/autonomy/` + `config/`; runs honor budget/quiet-hour/risk-tier gates and emit receipts.

rows.title: Agentic Memory Operation Controller (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP)
rows.problem: Self-evolving memory requires explicit, auditable action selection rather than implicit write behavior.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/agentic_memory_operation_controller.{ts,js}` + policy `config/agentic_memory_operation_policy.json`; user-specific operation candidates and approvals are stored in `memory/memory_ops/` with adaptive operation scoring in `adaptive/memory_ops/`; permanent action-selection logic and guardrails remain in `systems/memory/` + `config/`; every destructive op requires reversible receipts and rollback proof.

rows.title: Observational Compression + Progressive Disclosure Retrieval
rows.problem: Raw history growth increases token burn and retrieval latency unless observation compaction and staged loading are enforced.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/observational_compression_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `config/observational_compression_policy.json`; user-specific observations/summaries are persisted under `memory/observations/` with adaptive compression thresholds under `adaptive/observations/`; permanent compaction/index/search logic remains in `systems/memory/` + `config/` and enforces metadata-first retrieval before full-node expansion.

rows.title: MCP Interoperability + Skill Discovery Gateway
rows.problem: Ecosystem leverage requires standards-based tool/context interoperability without weakening existing signed gate posture.
rows.acceptance: Implement `skills/mcp/mcp_gateway.{ts,js}` + policy `config/mcp_gateway_policy.json` and CLI surface `protheusctl skills discover --mcp`; user-specific installed-skill preferences remain in `memory/skills/mcp/` with adaptive skill-quality scoring in `adaptive/skills/mcp/`; permanent protocol adapters and verification logic remain in `skills/mcp/` + `systems/security/` + `config/`.

rows.title: A2A Delegation Plane (Agent-to-Agent Protocol Contracts)
rows.problem: Cross-agent collaboration/delegation requires explicit contractized handoff semantics to avoid ad hoc bridge drift.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/a2a/a2a_delegation_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/a2a_delegation_policy.json`; user-specific trusted-peer preferences remain in `memory/federation/a2a/` with adaptive peer reliability scoring in `adaptive/federation/a2a/`; permanent delegation cards/handoff/auth contracts remain in `systems/a2a/` + `config/` with signed handoff receipts.

rows.title: System-3 Executive Layer (Growth Journal + Intrinsic Goal Loop)
rows.problem: Reflex/strategy loops are strong, but persistent narrative self-modeling and intrinsic-goal refinement need a dedicated executive layer.
rows.acceptance: Implement `adaptive/executive/system3_executive_layer.{ts,js}` + policy `config/system3_executive_policy.json`; user-specific growth-journal and executive preferences persist in `memory/executive/` + `adaptive/executive/`; permanent executive orchestration/governance logic remains in `adaptive/executive/` + `systems/autonomy/` + `config/`; layer runs continuously with bounded cadence and constitution-aligned veto gates.

rows.title: Ops Hardening Pack (Audit Chain + Background Hands + Skills UX)
rows.problem: Production-grade unattended operation needs immutable audit continuity, bounded always-on work execution, and low-friction operator tooling.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/audit/hash_chain_ledger.{ts,js}`, `systems/spine/background_hands_scheduler.{ts,js}`, and CLI integration in `systems/ops/protheusctl_skills_discover.{ts,js}` with policies; user-specific operator preferences live in `memory/ops/preferences/` and adaptive cadence/queue tuning under `adaptive/ops/`; permanent audit/scheduler/CLI contracts remain in `systems/` + `config/` with non-bypass integrity checks and rollback-safe controls.

rows.title: `protheus-core` Modular Extraction (Kernel Package Contract)
rows.problem: Core adoption and maintainability improve when the kernel is a strict minimal package and all higher layers are optional modules.
rows.acceptance: Implement `packages/protheus-core/` extraction with explicit public APIs for spine/reflex/gates; user-specific defaults/profiles remain in `memory/core_profiles/` and adaptive auto-tuning hints in `adaptive/core_profiles/`; permanent kernel package/runtime contracts remain in `packages/protheus-core/` + `systems/` + `config/`; installation/bootstrap supports one-command starter without leaking optional heavy layers.

rows.title: Spine Kernel Budget Gate (<600 LOC Orchestration-Only Contract)
rows.problem: Kernel growth erodes reliability and reasoning clarity; policy logic should live outside the orchestration spine.
rows.acceptance: Add `systems/ops/spine_kernel_budget_check.{ts,js}` + policy `config/spine_kernel_budget_policy.json`; enforce max-size + forbidden-policy-surface checks on spine entrypoints; user-specific run preferences remain in `memory/spine/preferences/` with adaptive orchestration hints in `adaptive/spine/`; permanent checks remain in `systems/ops/` + `config/` and fail closed in CI.

rows.title: Cyclic Strategy Graph Runner (Governed State-Graph Execution)
rows.problem: Complex long-running tasks need bounded cyclic graph execution contracts rather than ad hoc step chains.
rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/strategy/cyclic_strategy_graph_runner.{ts,js}` + policy `config/cyclic_strategy_graph_policy.json`; user-specific strategy graph preferences remain in `memory/strategy/graphs/` with adaptive transition weights in `adaptive/strategy/graphs/`; permanent graph runtime, node contracts, and guard rules remain in `systems/strategy/` + `config/`; includes deterministic cycle limits, retry budgets, and rollback receipts.

rows.title: Hierarchical Memory View Plane (Core/Edit/Summary + Swap-Safe Summarization)

rows.acceptance: Implement `systems/memory/hierarchical_memory_view_plane.{ts,js}` + policy `config/hierarchical_memory_view_policy.json`; user-specific view artifacts remain in `memory/views/` with adaptive view-selection heuristics in `adaptive/memory/views/`; permanent derivation/summarization contracts remain in `systems/memory/` + `config/`; model-swap path emits before/after parity receipts and bounded summary drift checks.

rows.title: Role-Based Crew Spawn Orchestrator (Shared Namespace Governance)
rows.problem: Multi-agent role execution is higher leverage when template-driven and governed by explicit shared-state/clearance boundaries.

rows.title: Competitive Observability + Benchmark Pack (Trace/Evals + Framework Parity)

rows.title: `protheus-core` Runtime Envelope Tightening (`<5MB`, `<200ms`, Optional-Flag Matrix)

rows.title: Deterministic Execution-Floor Bootstrap Lane (1 Shippable Outcome Minimum)

rows.acceptance: Add FreeBSD adapters for Ports/Poudriere publishing, ZFS boot environments, Capsicum/Jail confinement, PF/ipfw policy integration, GEOM/geli encryption hooks, and hardened kernel toggles; require verification receipts for each subsystem and include rollback-safe fallback to generic channel when parity tests fail.

rows.title: Raspberry Pi OS Edge Hardware Adaptation Pack (GPIO/Boot/Firmware/Provisioning/OTA)

rows.acceptance: Add Pi adapters for boot config/firmware control, GPIO/I2C/SPI/camera/HAT interfaces, thermal/battery-aware scheduling profiles, headless/desktop footprint variants, Pi Imager provisioning, and repository/OTA channels; require verification receipts for hardware-interface correctness and include rollback-safe fallback for unsupported firmware/hardware revisions.

rows.title: Alpine Minimal-Hardening Adaptation Pack (musl/apk/rootless scratch/read-only overlay)

rows.acceptance: Add Alpine adapters for musl/apk packaging, rootless scratch container optimization, read-only overlay deployment profiles, static/busybox compatibility, and secure mirror update channels; require verification receipts for footprint/security parity and include rollback-safe fallback to generic profile when constraints are unmet.

rows.acceptance: Implement unified signed artifact publishing adapters per distro channel with provenance and policy checks, require verification receipts for distribution integrity and channel parity, and include rollback-safe channel freeze/revert contracts for compromised or invalid artifacts.

rows.title: Zero-Touch Provisioning Matrix (MAAS/cloud-init/Pi Imager + Enterprise Bootstrap Contracts)
rows.problem: Fleet-scale adoption requires deterministic provisioning flows across server and edge hardware classes.
rows.acceptance: Add provisioning matrix adapters for zero-touch bootstrap workflows across supported ecosystems, require verification receipts for bootstrap parity/idempotence, and include rollback-safe provisioning halt/recovery controls on failed bootstrap validations.

rows.title: Cross-Ecosystem Certified Hardware & Tuning Matrix Lane
rows.problem: Production reliability depends on validated hardware classes with deterministic tuning and support envelopes.
rows.acceptance: Add certified hardware matrix and tuning profile contracts across target vendor classes and Pi tiers, require verification receipts for compatibility and tuning non-regression, and include rollback-safe fallback to generic hardware profiles on certification drift.

rows.title: Long-LTS Migration & Support Continuity Lane (Ubuntu/Enterprise Linux Focus)
rows.problem: Long-window enterprise operations need explicit migration continuity and support lifecycle controls to avoid version-support dead ends.
rows.acceptance: Add migration/support continuity lane covering LTS upgrade tooling and support-window policy checks, require verification receipts for migration safety and support compatibility, and include rollback-safe migration abort/revert controls on failed preflight or post-upgrade validation.

rows.title: Multi-Platform Linux/Unix Conformance Promotion Gate
rows.problem: Expanding distro coverage increases drift risk unless one consolidated conformance gate enforces adaptation/security/reliability invariants.
rows.acceptance: Add conformance gate enforcing host-profile match, immutable/atomic/rollback behavior, security policy checks (SELinux/eBPF/AppArmor/Capsicum where relevant), certified-hardware and air-gapped validation requirements, require verification receipts per promotion, and include rollback-safe promotion hold/fallback on gate instability.

rows.title: Neutral-Core Cross-Ecosystem Hardening Uplift (Post-Intake Consolidation)
rows.problem: Valuable patterns from ecosystem-specific lanes should become neutral defaults so future steward variants start from a stronger base.
rows.acceptance: Extend neutral-core backport pipeline to ingest approved cross-ecosystem controls (immutability patterns, declarative config contracts, minimal-footprint lanes, capability security defaults, hardware-certification checks, long-LTS lifecycle controls), require verification receipts for non-regression across existing steward profiles, and include rollback-safe opt-out toggles for incompatible environments.

rows.title: PlatformSocket ABI + Signed Manifest Contract
rows.problem: Adaptation channels provide host-aware behavior, but long-term extensibility requires one stable plugin ABI with auditable metadata and capability boundaries.
rows.acceptance: Added socket ABI/manifest contract implementation in `systems/ops/platform_socket_runtime.{ts,js}` backed by `config/platform_socket_registry.json`; manifest fields now enforce identity/version/module type, HostProfile predicates, capability claims, attestation signature metadata, and rollback-compatible activation receipts.

rows.title: Socket Discovery/Loader Runtime (Registry + Version Pin + Hot-Swap)
rows.problem: A socket ABI alone is insufficient without governed discovery and lifecycle controls that keep runtime behavior deterministic and fail-closed.
rows.acceptance: Implemented discover/activate lifecycle in `platform_socket_runtime` with deterministic socket selection from registry, fail-closed fallback to generic socket, signed lifecycle receipts, and persisted install/update/test state under `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/installed.json` plus audit history in `state/ops/platform_socket_runtime/history.jsonl`.

rows.title: GenericSocket + Named Host Socket Refactor (Channels -> Sockets)
rows.problem: Existing channel modules need a controlled refactor target so universal controls remain centralized while host-specific logic remains isolated and swappable.
rows.acceptance: Refactored adaptation representation into `config/platform_socket_registry.json` with `generic` plus named host sockets (`ubuntu`, `freebsd`, `nixos`, `raspios`, `alpine`), preserving host-predicate activation behavior and fallback semantics while keeping universal contracts centralized in the GenericSocket lane.

rows.title: Socket Lifecycle Operator Surface (`protheusctl socket *` + API)
rows.problem: Operators need deterministic governance for socket inventory, install/update policy, and pre-activation testing without direct filesystem edits.
rows.acceptance: Wired operator lifecycle surfaces through `systems/ops/protheusctl.ts` and `platform_socket_runtime` for `protheusctl socket list|install|update|test|status|discover|admission`, with receipted audit trails, signature-aware manifest checks, and rollback-safe state tracking for lifecycle operations.

rows.title: Socket Admission Proof Gate (Formal + Redteam + HostProfile Chaos Validation)
rows.problem: Community-installed sockets increase attack and reliability surface unless admission is blocked by explicit proof contracts and adversarial validation.
rows.acceptance: Added socket admission gate (`platform_socket_runtime admission`) with policy `config/platform_socket_admission_policy.json`, proof-stub requirements (`docs/proofs/platform/socket_*.md`), signature/capability validation, chaos eligibility checks, signed admission receipts, and fail/quarantine-ready outputs for non-compliant sockets.

rows.title: Eyes signal weighting retune in router ranking
rows.acceptance: Router eyes-signal scoring now supports ratio-scaled degraded/signal-rich weighting (`systems/routing/model_router.ts`) with tuned policy knobs in `config/agent_routing_rules.json`; regression coverage validates ratio-scaled local bonus behavior (`memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`).

rows.title: Prompt cache policy split by lane (reflex/autonomy/dream)
rows.acceptance: Router prompt-cache policy now supports per-lane overrides (`reflex`/`autonomy`/`dream`) with lane-keyed cache signatures and telemetry (`systems/routing/model_router.ts`, `config/agent_routing_rules.json`); regression coverage validates lane override behavior (`memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`).

rows.title: Dream fallback quality scoring before proposal injection
rows.acceptance: Proposal enrichment now computes dream-signal quality (`quality_score`, `quality_tier`, `quality_scale`) before applying dream bonuses, and low-quality signals are automatically scaled down with explicit reason codes (`systems/autonomy/proposal_enricher.ts`); coverage updated in `memory/tools/tests/proposal_enricher.test.js`.

rows.title: Proposal semantic dedupe (near-duplicate clustering)
rows.acceptance: Autonomy candidate intake now applies semantic near-duplicate suppression using token-stem Jaccard similarity with same-context guards (type + source/objective) before ranking (`systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`), and regression coverage validates strict vs relaxed context behavior (`memory/tools/tests/autonomy_semantic_dedupe.test.js`).

rows.title: Outcome correlation expansion (task-type x eye-source x model)
rows.acceptance: Router outcome learning now tracks and scores `task_type x source_eye x model` buckets (`by_task_eye_source`) alongside global/capability/task-type lanes, with source-eye context wired end-to-end from autonomy to route execution (`systems/autonomy/autonomy_controller.ts`, `systems/routing/route_execute.ts`, `systems/routing/route_task.ts`, `systems/routing/model_router.ts`) and regression coverage in `memory/tools/tests/model_router_routing_features.test.js`.

rows.title: Focus-controller trigger adaptation cadence tuning
rows.acceptance: Focus controller now computes adaptive cadence (`systems/sensory/focus_controller.ts`) from recent focus pressure + realized outcome score, applies effective refresh windows (trigger + lens) with min-interval anti-thrash guards, and emits cadence telemetry in refresh/status payloads. Policy/store defaults were extended in `systems/adaptive/sensory/eyes/focus_trigger_store.ts` and regression coverage added in `memory/tools/tests/focus_controller.test.js`.
